# WI-QUIC-API-0007 - Incursa.Quic Public Stream-Transfer Comparison Work Item

## Summary

Add a bounded BenchmarkDotNet suite for matched public bidirectional request/response stream-transfer workloads against Incursa.Quic and `System.Net.Quic`.

## Requirements Addressed

- REQ-QUIC-API-0016

## Design Inputs

- ARC-QUIC-API-0007

## Planned Changes

- Add or extend a permanent BenchmarkDotNet suite that runs matched loopback public-facade bidirectional request/response stream-transfer workloads against Incursa.Quic and `System.Net.Quic`.
- Keep the measured stream workload at the public listener, connection, and stream facade level for both stacks.
- Share or align certificate generation, client trust setup, ALPN, loopback endpoint allocation, stream direction, request payload size, response payload size, timeouts, and disposal cleanup.
- Omit unsupported implementations via each stack's public `IsSupported` markers instead of synthesizing placeholder benchmark results.
- Preserve the existing connection-only comparison as a separate `REQ-QUIC-API-0015` result and document the new stream-transfer reports separately.

## Out of Scope

- Changing Incursa public API shape.
- Comparing internal helper, parser, recovery, congestion, packet, or stream-state benchmarks to public `System.Net.Quic` workloads.
- HTTP/3, interop-runner, internet, retry, transfer-harness, 0-RTT, key-update, or broader protocol throughput claims.
- Weakening stream completion, EOF, cancellation, or cleanup behavior to improve benchmark numbers.
- Merging connection-only and stream-transfer results into one undifferentiated benchmark claim.

## Verification Plan

Before benchmark implementation, run focused public API requirement-home tests for `REQ-QUIC-API-0010` to prove the Incursa public request/response stream workload still executes through the public facade. When the benchmark suite lands, run BenchmarkDotNet `Dry` to validate harness shape, then `Short` for a local repeatable baseline, preserve the exported reports, and finish with SpecTrace render/check, `git diff --check`, and the known-baseline core SpecTrace validation profile.

## Completion Notes

Completed the permanent public-facade loopback request/response stream-transfer benchmark suite and preserved Dry plus Short evidence under `artifacts/verification/20260424-public-api-stream-transfer-comparison/`. The comparison now covers the bounded public request/response workload on both Incursa.Quic and `System.Net.Quic`.
